parsed_review.summary: CP00-490 is a descriptor-only RP16 governance closeout pack (150 units, RP16.P03.M06.S13-RP16.P04.M03.S20, risk C) and passes read-only review with no findings. Distribution reconciles exactly across the prompt baseline, review-baseline.json included_units, manifest.json, registry.js GOVERNANCE_CORE_CP490_REQUIREMENTS, and the validate-rp16-governance-core-contract.mjs assertions (deliverable {test:13,hermes_evidence:7,claude_review:11,implementation:53,contract:17,security_audit:16,ui:30,fixture:3}; phases P03=80/P04=70; 9 sections; next CP00-491 @ RP16.P04.M03.S21). The pack-level claim is honestly scoped: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status implemented_pending_review, flag governance_core_p03_closeout_p04_foundation_descriptor_verified, with enterprise trust explicitly deferred to human approval. No-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: grep of packages/governance found no write/network/child_process/db/exec patterns (only read-only validation fs use), and the extensive no_write attestation flags (writes_product_state=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, no_real_data=true) match the frozen descriptor objects. Claude remains a read-only, non-final-approver reviewer (authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, claude packet read_only=true, tools Read/Grep/Glob) and validators enforce these invariants. Hermes patterns are used only for the deterministic review packet/evidence summary, not product runtime. Review-hardening checks are satisfied: auth preflight passed with valid JSON, invalid_attempts_preserved=[], and unrelated dirty files (control-room UI, c-ldip-01 invalid attempt) are correctly excluded from pack scope. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are referenced but not yet present, which is the expected state for production_ready=false and must be generated and re-validated, with human final approval, before commit and any enterprise-trust claim.
